reasoning: Q3 is 12.0x slower than DuckDB (936.5ms vs 77.8ms), with a clear bottleneck: orders_filter_join (725ms, 77.4% of total) is single-threaded on a 64-core system. Parallelizing this phase with morsel-driven execution should yield ~64x speedup (725ms → 11ms). Combined with zone map pruning (skip 51% of blocks) and partial_sort for LIMIT 10, total query time could drop from 936ms to ~100-150ms (6-9x improvement), approaching DuckDB's performance. Two iterations remain; this is a high-confidence optimization path.
focus_areas: Parallelize orders_filter_join scan across 64 cores using morsel-driven execution (main bottleneck at 77.4%)
Implement zone map pruning for orders.o_orderdate to skip ~51% of blocks with min_orderdate >= 9204
Replace std::sort with std::partial_sort for LIMIT 10 in final result ordering
strategy_notes: Attack the dominant bottleneck (orders_filter_join) aggressively with parallelism. The 64-core system is severely underutilized (single-threaded). Use the morsel-driven execution pattern already implemented for lineitem scan. Simultaneously enable zone map pruning to reduce data volume. Finally, fix the sort phase to use O(n log K) instead of O(n log n). These three optimizations have low risk and high impact. If this iteration succeeds, GenDB should reach 100-200ms range. For iteration 2, consider compact hash tables and aggregation tuning if needed.
notes: Orders table is 15M rows; with 64 threads and 100K morsel size, expect near-linear scaling. Zone map file may need generation during data load if not present. Partial_sort requires C++11; ensure compiler supports std::min(). Target: 100-200ms total (6-9x improvement) by end of iteration 1.
